enriched_points.expanded_content: Regenerative therapies often face protracted approval timelines, complicating access and increasing costs, ultimately delaying patient treatment options.
Establishing uniform protocols across clinical trials is essential to ensure reliable outcomes and comparability of data, yet current practices vary widely.
Regulatory hurdles can restrict patient access to promising therapies, particularly in underserved populations, exacerbating healthcare disparities.
Regulators strive to foster innovative treatments while ensuring patient safety, creating tension between rapid development and comprehensive evaluation.
Anticipated changes in regulatory frameworks may streamline processes, but ongoing adjustments will be required to keep pace with rapid advancements in regenerative medicine.
